zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor Beauchamp於1408年9月出生在英國瓦維其可爾時，她的父親Richard Beauchamp27歲，母親Elizabeth de Berkeley22歲。她於1426年與Edmund Beaufort Holland 2nd Duke of Somerset在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1467年3月6日在英國倫敦享年58歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 包利斯 凱德拉爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eleanor Beauchamp于1408年9月出生在英国瓦維其可爾时，她的父亲Richard Beauchamp27岁，母亲Elizabeth de Berkeley22岁。她于1426年与Edmund Beaufort Holland 2nd Duke of Somerset在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1467年3月6日在英国倫敦享年58岁，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦聖 包利斯 凱德拉爾。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor Beauchamp was born in September 1408, in Warwickshire, England, her father, Richard Beauchamp, was 27 and her mother, Elizabeth de Berkeley, was 22. She married Edmund Beaufort Holland 2nd Duke of Somerset in 1426, in Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 6 March 1467, in London, England, at the age of 58, and was buried in St Paul's Cathedral, London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eleanor Beauchamp est née en septembre 1408, à Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Beauchamp, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Berkeley, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Edmund Beaufort Holland 2nd Duke of Somerset en 1426, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 6 mars 1467, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterrée à St Paul's Cathedral, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hat 1426 in Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom Edmund Beaufort Holland 2nd Duke of Somerset geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 6. ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor Beauchampが1408年9月にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアで生まれた時，父Richard Beauchampは27歳，母Elizabeth de Berkeleyは22歳であった。1426年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアでEdmund Beaufort Holland 2nd Duke of Somersetと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1467年3月6日にイギリスロンドンで58歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンスト プアウル'ス カスエドラルにて埋葬された。

ko.St Paul's Cathedral, London, England: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 폴'스 캐테드럴
ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor Beauchamp(이)가 1408년 9월 영국 워윅실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Beauchamp(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth de Berkeley(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1426년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르에서 Edmund Beaufort Holland 2nd Duke of Somerset(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1467년 3월 6일 영국 런던에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 폴'스 캐테드럴에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Edmund Beaufort Holland 2nd Duke of Somerset em 1426, em Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 6 filhas. ru.St Paul's Cathedral, London, England: ст поул'с кэтедраль, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor Beauchamp родилась сентябрь 1408 г. в местности варвиккшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Beauchamp было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Berkeley, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Edmund Beaufort Holland 2nd Duke of Somerset в 1426 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 6 марта 1467 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 58 лет и была похоронена в местности ст поул'с кэтедраль, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.
